name: time-series
context: Work with pandas time series: to_datetime, DateTimeIndex, time zones, offsets, resample, rolling windows, shift, and asfreq
info: Pandas time-series workflows cover timestamp parsing, timezone normalization, DateTimeIndex construction, resampling into fixed frequency buckets, rolling calculations, lag features, and calendar-aware offsets. Agents use this node for event logs, metrics, billing periods, daily active counts, monthly revenue, and temporal feature engineering. It emphasizes parsing external timestamps with `utc=True`, sorting by time, choosing offset aliases deliberately, handling invalid dates, and using the shared `resample_timeseries.py` script for repeatable file-to-file summaries.
body:
# Time-Series Workflows

Time-series correctness starts with timestamp parsing. Normalize external timestamps to UTC, sort by time, then resample or roll.

## Parse and resample

```python
import pandas as pd

events['ts'] = pd.to_datetime(events['ts'], errors='coerce', utc=True)
daily = (
    events
    .dropna(subset=['ts'])
    .set_index('ts')
    .sort_index()
    .resample('D')
    .agg(events=('event_id', 'count'), users=('user_id', 'nunique'))
    .reset_index()
)
```

## Per-entity monthly summary

```python
monthly = (
    orders
    .assign(paid_at=lambda d: pd.to_datetime(d['paid_at'], errors='coerce', utc=True))
    .dropna(subset=['paid_at'])
    .set_index('paid_at')
    .groupby('region')
    .resample('ME')
    .agg(revenue=('amount', 'sum'), orders=('order_id', 'nunique'))
    .reset_index()
)
```

## CLI resample

```bash
python nodes/pandas/workflows/scripts/resample_timeseries.py orders.csv monthly.parquet --date-column paid_at --freq ME --group-by region --agg amount:sum --utc
```

sections.title: Parsing timestamps
sections.body: Use `pd.to_datetime(column, errors='coerce', utc=True)` for external timestamps. `errors='coerce'` turns invalid timestamps into `NaT` so they can be counted and handled. Keep original strings if parsing failures need audit. Convert to local time for presentation with `.dt.tz_convert(...)` after UTC normalization.

sections.title: Resampling
sections.body: Set a DateTimeIndex, sort it, then call `.resample(freq).agg(...)`. Common offset aliases include `D` for day, `W` for week, `ME` for month end, `MS` for month start, `QE` for quarter end, `h` for hour, and `15min` for fixed intervals. Use [[pandas/workflows/groupby]] with `groupby(...).resample(...)` for per-entity buckets.

sections.title: Rolling and shift
sections.body: `rolling(window)` computes moving statistics. Use row-count windows such as `rolling(7)` or time windows such as `rolling('7D')` on a time index. `shift()` creates lags and period-over-period comparisons. Sort by timestamp and group before shifting entity-specific series.

sections.title: Frequency and gaps
sections.body: `asfreq()` conforms a time index to a frequency without aggregation. Use it to reveal missing intervals, then fill deliberately with `ffill`, `bfill`, or numeric zeros only when the domain supports it. Do not fill missing revenue or event counts without distinguishing no activity from missing data.

scripts.name: Resample Time Series
scripts.description: Parse a timestamp column, optionally group by dimensions, resample by a pandas offset alias, and aggregate values

frequency.description: Pandas offset alias such as D, W, ME, h, 15min

depends.reason: Timestamp columns need parsing and invalid-date policy
